scene: A snooker match at what is likely the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield -- home of the World Championship. The match appears to be in a deciding frame, meaning both players are level and this final frame determines the winner. A player is at the table with what the commentator describes as a makeable chance.

glossary.definition: The final frame of a match when both players are level; whoever wins it wins the match.
glossary.source_quote: just one chance in the deciding frame and this is a potential chance

narratives.title: Deciding frame chance at the table
narratives.summary: A player has a chance on the table in the deciding frame. The commentator suggests it is a makeable opportunity and the player must convert.

key_moments.what: Deciding frame, player at table with a chance
key_moments.why_it_matters: In snooker a deciding frame is sudden death -- whoever wins this frame wins the entire match.

key_moments.what: Commentator says the shot is among the easiest available and must be potted
key_moments.why_it_matters: Emphasis on 'must pot' signals a straightforward ball the player cannot afford to miss under this pressure.

do_not_say.reason: Whisper artifact -- not a snooker term; underlying word unclear, possibly 'players' or a player name
Repeated Whisper artifacts across [30s]–[37s]; underlying snooker terms (likely 'shots', 'pot') are unclear
